title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in layered parser-to-browser verification matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.
Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Test Pyramid
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 6422 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-validation-browser-nan052
# Competency: ssg-validation-browser.
# Case 2609: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_validation_browser_nan052(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_nan052 = "nan052-250nan-2609"
    assert evidence_variant_nan052
    evidence_axes_nan052 = (
        "readernan052",
        "source2609",
        "parse250nan",
        "routenan0522609",
        "artifact2609nan052",
        "recovery250nan2609",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_nan052)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"
